optimistic_edit_without_read:RECURRING:STABLE — unit 8: Edit on plan.md (success=false) before rg-via-Bash anchor + Read + successful Edit; unit 34: Edit on file-select.v2.tsx (success=false) before /usr/bin/grep anchor + successful Edit; two instances consistent with all prior eight measured cycles at 1-2 instances per cycle; no improvement trend across 10+ consecutive cycles

violations: unit 8: Edit(success=false) on plan.md without prior Read — assertion that old_string was present without verifying file state
unit 34: Edit(success=false) on file-select.v2.tsx without prior Read — same failure mode
unit 4: Read(success=false) on use-sync-q.ts — assumed path without observation
unit 46: Read(success=false) on use-sync-q.ts — same assumption repeated within same session

cross_references.insight: Any value that acts as a system identifier must be read from its canonical source before use, never inferred from naming patterns
violations: unit 4: inferred use-sync-q.ts path from naming convention (use-q.ts, use-query-params.ts) rather than listing directory first
unit 46: same inference repeated despite same failure earlier in session — canonical source is src/utils/hooks/use-query-sync/ directory, not a flat file

confirmations: unit 8 re_edit_thrash on plan.md with 7+ Edit calls matches the pattern where root-cause understanding is lost mid-task, causing repeated partial fixes — consistent with the rule that iteration 3+ of a fix loop should trigger core-dump before continuing
cross_references.insight: Context limit hits correlate with fix-thrash loops

recommendations: path_assumption_bias has now repeated the double-unit intra-session pattern for three consecutive sessions — this warrants structural intervention: add a Glob call before any Read on hook files with ambiguous paths rather than assuming the flat file exists at root level

rm_instead_of_trash has now occurred in three consecutive sessions with identical self-correction pattern — the hook is catching it correctly but the initial reflex is not being suppressed; a pre-tool hook that rewrites rm to trash would eliminate the violation entirely rather than catching it post-facto

shell_tool_preference_violations and re_edit_thrash recommendations deprioritized per instructions — both show zero improvement trend across 10+ cycles with no change in severity or frequency; further recommendations without structural enforcement mechanism are not expected to produce different outcomes
